question: Calculate the energy stored in capacitor C when C = 100 μF and U = 30 V.
Calculate the capacitance C of the capacitor, given that it stores Q = 3 mC when fully charged under U = 30 V.

Two charges, q1 = 6 × 10^-8 C and q2 = -6 × 10^-8 C, are placed at points A and B in air, 8 cm apart. A third charge, q3 = 6 × 10^-8 C, is placed at point C, with CA = 5 cm and CB = 3 cm. Determine the force acting on q3.

Three electric charges are placed at three fixed points, forming a right-angled triangle ABC (right-angled at A), where AB = 4 m and BC = 5 m. The charges are qA = 5.0 μC, qB = -5.0 μC, and qC = 4.0 μC, respectively. Find the net electric force acting on the charge at A.

Points A and B are separated by 20 cm in air. Charges q1 = -3 × 10^-6 C and q2 = 8 × 10^-6 C are placed at A and B, respectively. A test charge q3 = 2 × 10^-6 C is placed at point C such that AC = 12 cm and BC = 16 cm. Calculate the magnitude of the electric force acting on q3.

Two point charges q1 = 3 × 10^-8 C and q2 = 2 × 10^-8 C are placed at points A and B in a vacuum, with AB = 5 cm. A charge q0 = -2 × 10^-8 C is placed at M, where MA = 4 cm and MB = 3 cm. Calculate the net electric force acting on q0.

Three electric charges, q1 = q2 = q3 = 1.6 × 10^-19 C, are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle ABC with side length 16 cm in air. Determine the net electric force vector acting on q3.

Two point charges q1 = 10^-8 C and q2 = -3×10^-8 C are placed in air at two points A and B, 8 cm apart. A point charge q = 10^-8 C is placed at point M, which is on the perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB and 3 cm away from AB. Given k = 9×10^9 N.m^2/C^2. What is the magnitude of the net electric force exerted by q1 and q2 on q?

Two charges, q1 = +2×10^-6 C and q2 = -2×10^-6 C, are placed at points A and B in a vacuum, 6 cm apart. A third charge, q3 = +2×10^-6 C, is placed on the perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB, 4 cm away from AB. What is the magnitude of the net electric force exerted by q1 and q2 on q3?
Two electric forces are acting in the same direction, with magnitudes of 3 N and 4 N respectively. What is the resultant force?
Two electric forces, each with a magnitude of 5 N, act at an angle of 60° to each other. What is the resultant force?

Three charges q1 = +2 μC, q2 = +2 μC, and q3 = -2 μC are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle with a side length of 10 cm. Calculate the magnitude of the net electric force acting on q3.

An electric charge is subjected to two electric forces with magnitudes of 4 N and 3 N, acting at an angle of 90° to each other. Calculate the resultant force. (This is a very direct and clear translation.)
Three charges q1 = +1 μC, q2 = +1 μC, and q3 = -1 μC are placed at the vertices of an equilateral triangle with side a = 20 cm. Calculate the magnitude of the net force acting on q3.

Two charges are placed equidistant from point O. The electric forces exerted by these two charges on point O are 6 N and 8 N, respectively, and are perpendicular to each other. Calculate the resultant force at O.
Two charges separated by 15 cm exert a force of 4.8 N. Given that q1 = q2 = q, find q.
Two electric forces with magnitudes of 5 N and 12 N act at an angle of 60° to each other. Calculate the resultant force.

Three identical charges q = +1 μC are placed at the three vertices of an isosceles right triangle, with sides of length a = 10 cm. Calculate the net force acting on the charge at the right angle vertex.
Three charges: q1 = +3 μC, q2 = -2 μC, and q3 = +1 μC are placed 10 cm apart on a straight line. Calculate the force acting on q2.

Two identical charges q = +2 μC are placed at two vertices of an equilateral triangle with side length a = 0.1 m. A charge q′ = -1 μC is placed at the remaining vertex. Calculate the net electric force acting on q′.
Two electric forces have magnitudes of 6 N and 8 N, acting at an angle of 120° to each other. Calculate the resultant force of these two forces.
A charge is acted upon by two forces, each of magnitude 10 N. Find the angle between the two forces if the resultant force is also 10 N.

A charge q = -1 μC is attracted by two +1 μC charges. These two positive charges are located on opposite sides of q, along the same straight line passing through q, at distances of 5 cm and 12 cm respectively from q. Calculate the magnitude of the net electric force acting on q.
Two electric charges, q1 = +2 μC and q2 = -2 μC, are placed 10 cm apart. A charge q3 = +1 μC is placed at the midpoint of the line segment connecting q1 and q2. Calculate the net force acting on q3.
Two forces act on a charge. They are collinear and act in opposite directions, with magnitudes of 15 N and 9 N. Calculate the magnitude and direction of the resultant force.
Three charges q1 = +1 μC, q2 = -2 μC, q3 = +3 μC are placed at the vertices of an isosceles right triangle with legs of 0.1 m. Calculate the net force acting on q3.

Two charges, q1 = +2 μC and q2 = +2 μC, are placed at the ends of a 10 cm long line segment. A third charge, q3 = -1 μC, is positioned along the line connecting q1 and q2. Calculate the net force acting on q3 when it is 4 cm away from q1.

Two electric charges, q1 = 8 × 10^-8 C and q2 = -8 × 10^-8 C, are placed at points A and B respectively, in air (AB = 6 cm). Determine the force acting on a third charge q3 = 8 × 10^-8 C, given that: CA = 4 cm and CB = 2 cm.
Two charges, q1 = 8×10^-8 C and q2 = -8×10^-8 C, are placed at points A and B in the air (AB = 6 cm). Determine the force acting on a third charge q3 = 8×10^-8 C, if CA = 4 cm and CB = 10 cm.

Two point charges, q1 = 8 × 10⁻⁸ C and q2 = -8 × 10⁻⁸ C, are placed at points A and B, respectively, in air (AB = 6 cm). Determine the force acting on a third charge q3 = 8 × 10⁻⁸ C if it is placed at point C such that CA = CB = 5 cm.

Three point charges q1 = 4×10^-8 C, q2 = -4×10^-8 C, and q3 = 5×10^-8 C are placed in air at the three vertices A, B, C of an equilateral triangle with side a = 2 cm. Determine the magnitude of the force vector acting on q3.

Three charges, q1 = 8×10^-9 C, q2 = q3 = -8×10^-9 C, are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle ABC with side length a = 6 cm, in air. Determine the net force acting on a charge q0 = 6×10^-9 C placed at the center O of the triangle.

In a vacuum, consider two electric charges q1 = -q2 = 10^-7 C placed at points A and B, separated by 8 cm. Determine the resultant force acting on electric charge q0 = 10^-7 C when q0 is placed at H, which is the midpoint of AB.

In a vacuum, two charges q1 = 10^-7 C and q2 = -10^-7 C are placed at points A and B, separated by 8 cm. Determine the resultant force acting on a third charge q0 = 10^-7 C when q0 is placed at point M, 4 cm from A and 12 cm from B.

Three charges are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle with side length a = 6 cm, in air: q1 = 6×10^-9 C, q2 = q3 = -8×10^-9 C. Determine the net force acting on a charge q0 = 8×10^-9 C placed at the center of the triangle.

Two point charges q1 = 2×10⁻² µC and q2 = -2×10⁻² µC are placed at points A and B, separated by a distance a = 30 cm in air. What is the magnitude of the electric force exerted on a charge qo = 2×10⁻⁹ C placed at point M, which is equidistant from A and B by a distance equal to 'a'?

Two point charges q1 = 4×10^-8 C and q2 = -4×10^-8 C are placed at points A and B, separated by a distance of 4 cm in air. What is the force acting on a charge q = 2×10^-7 C placed at the midpoint O of AB?

Two point charges, q1 and q2, of equal magnitude and the same sign, are placed in air a distance r apart. A third point charge, q3, is placed at the midpoint of the line segment connecting q1 and q2. What is the net force acting on charge q3?

There are two charges, q1 = 2×10^-6 C and q2 = -2×10^-6 C, placed at points A and B respectively in a vacuum, separated by a distance of 6 cm. A third charge, q3 = 4×10^-6 C, is placed on the perpendicular bisector of AB, at a distance of 4 cm from the line segment AB. What is the magnitude of the net electric force exerted by charges q1 and q2 on charge q3?

At the three vertices of right-angled triangle ABC (right-angled at A), with AB = 30 cm, AC = 40 cm, and BC = 50 cm, charges q1 = q2 = q3 = 10^-9 C are placed. Determine the magnitude of the net electric force acting on a charge q = 10^-9 C placed at point H, which is the foot of the altitude from A.

Four identical charges q1, q2, q3, q4 are placed at the four vertices of a square ABCD, with a side length of 4 cm, respectively. Find the electric force acting on a charge q0 = 2×10^-8 C placed at the center of the square.
Two point charges q1 = 3 μC and q2 = -2 μC are placed at points A and B, separated by 6 cm. Find the magnitude of the electric force acting on q1 by q2? (Take k = 9×10^9 N×m²/C²)

Two charges of the same magnitude q are placed at two adjacent vertices of an isosceles right triangle with side length a = 10 cm. Find the magnitude of the total electric force acting on a test charge q0 placed at the remaining vertex, given F0 = kqq0/a^2.

Two charges, q1 = +1 μC and q2 = +1 μC, are placed at points A and B, separated by 4 cm. Find the net force acting on a test charge q0 = +2 μC placed at point M, which is located precisely at the midpoint between A and B.
Three identical charges 'q' are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle with side length 'a'. Find the net electric force acting on a test charge placed at the center of the triangle?

Two point charges q1 = +2 μC and q2 = -2 μC are placed at two points A and B, separated by 6 cm. A test charge q0 = +1 μC is placed at point M, located on the extension of line AB, 4 cm from A and 10 cm from B. Calculate the magnitude of the net force acting on q0?

Two point charges, q1 = +1 μC and q2 = -4 μC, are placed 6 cm apart. Point M is located on the line segment connecting the two charges, 2 cm away from qCalculate the magnitude of the net force acting on a test charge placed at M, if q0 = +1 μC.

Two electric charges q1 = +2 μC and q2 = +3 μC are placed at two points 5 cm apart. A test charge q0 = +1 μC is placed at a point 3 cm from q1 and 4 cm from q2. What is the net electric force acting on q0? (Use k = 9×10^9)

A test charge is placed at a point whose distances to the two charges q1 = +2 μC and q2 = -3 μC are 3 cm and 4 cm, respectively. The two charges are fixed and separated by 7 cm. What is the direction of the net electric force acting on the test charge?
A charge q1 = +2 μC is at A and q2 = -2 μC is at B, separated by 10 cm. A test charge is placed at the midpoint of AB. Calculate the net force acting on the test charge? If q0 = +1 μC.

Two charges, q1 = +5 μC and q2 = +5 μC, are placed at points A and B, 10 cm apart. Calculate the magnitude of the force acting on a test charge q0 = +1 μC placed at a point 6 cm from A and 8 cm from B.

Two charges, q1 = +1 μC and q2 = -1 μC, are placed at two points 10 cm apart. A test charge q0 = +2 μC is placed at a point equidistant from the two charges and located on the line connecting q1 and q2. What is the magnitude of the net electric force acting on q0?

Two point charges, q1 = q2 = 16 × 10⁻⁸ C, are placed at points A and B, which are 10 cm apart in the air. Determine the electric field strength (or intensity) caused by these two point charges at point N, where NA = 5 cm and NB = 15 cm.

Two point charges, q1 = q2 = 16 × 10⁻⁸ C, are placed at points A and B, which are 10 cm apart in air. Determine the electric field strength produced by these two point charges at point C, given that AC = BC = 8 cm.

At two points A and B, separated by 10 cm in the air, two charges q1 = -q2 = 6 × 10^-6 C are placed. Determine the electric field strength caused by these two point charges at point C, knowing that AC = BC = 12 cm.

Two point charges, q1 = 6 × 10^-6 C and q2 = -6 × 10^-6 C, are placed in air at points A and B, separated by 10 cm.
 Calculate the electric force exerted on a charge q3 = -3 × 10^-8 C placed at C.

At two points A and B, separated by 10 cm in air, two electric charges q1 = q2 = 16 × 10^-8 C are placed. Determine the electrostatic force acting on charge q3 = 2 × 10^-6 C, which is placed at point C. Given that AC = BC = 8 cm.

At two points A and B, separated by 20 cm in air, two point charges q1 = 4 × 10^-6 C and q2 = -6.4 × 10^-6 C are placed. Determine the electric field strength caused by these two point charges at point C, knowing that AC = 12 cm and BC = 16 cm. Determine the electric force acting on charge q3 = -5 × 10^-8 C placed at C.
At two points A and B, 20 cm apart in the air, two charges q1 = 4 × 10^-6 C and q2 = -6.4 × 10^-6 C are placed. Determine the electric force acting on a charge q3 = -5 × 10^-8 C placed at C.

Two charges, q1 = -1.6 × 10^-6 C and q2 = -2.4 × 10^-6 C, are placed at points A and B, respectively, which are 10 cm apart in air. Determine the electric field strength at point C due to these two charges. Given that AC = 8 cm and BC = 6 cm.

Four charges of the same magnitude q are placed at the four vertices of a square ABCD with side length a. Positive charges are located at A and C, while negative charges are located at B and D. Determine the resultant electric field strength at the intersection point of the square's diagonals.

Two electric charges, q1 = 5 × 10^-9 C and q2 = –5 × 10^-9 C, are placed 10 cm apart in a vacuum. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at the midpoint of the line connecting the two charges?

Two electric charges, q1 = q2 = 5 × 10^-16 C, are placed at vertices B and C of an equilateral triangle ABC with a side length of 8 cm, in air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at vertex A?

Two electric charges q1 = 5 × 10^-9 C and q2 = –5 × 10^-9 C are placed at two points separated by 10 cm in a vacuum. Calculate the magnitude of the electric field strength at a point located on the straight line passing through the two charges, and which is 5 cm from q1 and 15 cm from q2.

Two electric charges, q1 = 5 × 10^-16 C and q2 = –5 × 10^-16 C, are placed at vertices B and C of an equilateral triangle ABC with sides of 8 cm in the air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at vertex A of triangle ABC?
Two point charges, q1 = 0.5 nC and q2 = –0.5 nC, are placed at points A and B, 6 cm apart in air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at the midpoint of AB?

Two point charges, q1 = 0.5 nC and q2 = –0.5 nC, are placed at two points A and B, separated by 6 cm in air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at point M, which is located on the perpendicular bisector of AB, at a distance ℓ = 4 cm from the midpoint of AB?

Two point charges q1 = 2 × 10⁻² μC and q2 = –2 × 10⁻² μC are placed at two points A and B, separated by a distance a = 30 cm, in air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at point M, which is equidistant from A and B by a distance a = 30 cm?

Determine the electric field vector produced by a system of two point charges, q1 = 2 × 10^-7 C and q2 = -4 × 10^-7 C, at the midpoint of the line segment connecting the two charges. The two charges are separated by 10 cm and are located in alcohol, which has a dielectric constant ε = 2.2.

Three equal positive point charges, q = 5 × 10^-9 C, are placed at three vertices of a square with side length a = 40 cm. What is the magnitude of the electric field at the fourth vertex of the square?

Two charges, q1 = 4 × 10^-10 C and q2 = -4 × 10^-10 C, are placed at points A and B in the air, with the distance AB = a = 2 cm. Determine the electric field vector at point N such that A, B, and N form an equilateral triangle.

Two point charges q1 = -10^-6 C and q2 = 10^-6 C are placed at points A and B, separated by 40 cm in air. What is the resultant electric field strength at the midpoint M of AB? 
Hai điện tích điểm q1 = -10^-6 C, q2 = 10^-6 C đặt tại hai điểm A and B cách nhau 40 cm trong No khí. Cường độ điện trường tổng hợp tại trung điểm M của AB là.

Two point charges q1 = -10^-6 C and q2 = 10^-6 C are placed at two points A and B, 40 cm apart in a vacuum. What is the magnitude of the resultant electric field at point N, which is 20 cm from A and 60 cm from B?
Hai điện tích điểm q1 = -10^-6 and q2 = 10^-6 C đặt tại hai điểm A and B cách nhau 40 cm trong chân No. Cường độ điện trường tổng hợp tại điểm N cách A 20 cm and cách B 60 cm Yes độ lớn.

Two point charges, q1 = 0.5 nC and q2 = −0.5 nC, are placed at points A and B respectively. The points are separated by a distance a = 6 cm in the air. Determine the electric field strength E at point M, where M is the midpoint of AB.

There are two point charges, q1 = 0.5 nC and q2 = −0.5 nC, respectively placed at points A and B, separated by a distance a = 6 cm in the air. Determine the electric field strength E at point C, which is 6 cm from A and 12 cm from B.

There are two point charges q1 = 0.5 nC and q2 = −0.5 nC, respectively placed at points A and B, separated by a distance a = 6 cm in air. Determine the electric field strength E at point M, which is located on the perpendicular bisector of AB and 4 cm away from AB.

Two charges, q1 = 8 × 10^-9 C and q2 = −2 × 10^-9 C, are placed at points A and B, separated by 9 cm in a vacuum. Let C be the point where the net electric field is zero. What is the distance from A to C?

At the three vertices A, B, and C of a square ABCD with side length 6 cm in a vacuum, three point charges are placed: q1 = q3 = 2 × 10^-7 C and q2 = -4 × 10^-7 C. Determine the charge q4 placed at D such that the net electric field strength caused by the system of charges at the center O is zero.

Two test charges q1 and q2 (q1 = 4q2) are placed at points A and B respectively in an electric field. The force acting on q1 is F1, and the force acting on q2 is F2 (with F1 = 3F2). Let E1 and E2 be the electric field strengths at A and B respectively. What is the relationship between E1 and E2?

Two points A and B lie on the same electric field line generated by a positive point charge q > 0. The magnitude of the electric field strength at A is 36 V/m, and at B is 9 V/m. Determine the electric field strength at M, the midpoint of AB.
Two electric charges, q1 = q2 = 5 × 10^-9 C, are placed 10 cm apart in a vacuum. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at the midpoint of the line segment connecting the two charges?

Two electric charges, q1 = q2 = 5 × 10^-16 C, are placed at vertices B and C of an equilateral triangle ABC with a side length of 8 cm, in air. What is the magnitude of the electric field strength at vertex A of the triangle?

Point charges qA and qB, both equal to 3 × 10⁻⁷ C, are placed respectively at two points A and B in the air. The distance AB is 12 cm. M is a point located on the perpendicular bisector of AB, 8 cm away from the line segment AB. What is the magnitude of the resultant electric field strength produced by qA and qB at point M?

At two points A and B, separated by 8 cm in a vacuum, there are two point charges q1 = q2 = 16 × 10^-8 C. Determine the magnitude of the electric field caused by these two charges at point C, given that AC = BC = 8 cm.

Three positive charges, q1 = q2 = q3 = q = 5 × 10^-9 C, are placed at three consecutive vertices of a square with side length a = 30 cm in the air. What is the magnitude of the electric field at the fourth vertex?
Three identical charges Q are fixed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle with side 'a'. What is the magnitude of the electric field intensity at the center of the triangle?

At the three vertices of a right isosceles triangle ABC, with AB = AC = a, three positive charges qA = qB = q and qC = 2q are placed in a vacuum. What is the expression for the electric field intensity at H, which is the foot of the altitude dropped from the right-angle vertex A to the hypotenuse BC?
Two point charges, q1 = 4 mC and q2 = −9 mC, are placed at points A and B, separated by 9 cm in a vacuum. What is the distance from point M to B, given that the net electric field at point M is zero?
Given a square ABCD with side length 'a'. Charges q1 = q3 = q are placed at A and C. What charge must be placed at B so that the electric field at D is zero?

Given two electric charges q1 = 4.1 × 10^-10 C and q2 = −4.1 × 10^-10 C, located at points A and B in the air, separated by a distance AB = 2 cm. Determine the magnitude of the electric field strength E (V/m) at H, the midpoint of AB.

Given two electric charges q1 = 4.1 × 10^-10 C and q2 = −4.1 × 10^-10 C, placed at points A and B in air, with a separation AB = 2 cm. Determine the magnitude of the electric field strength E (in V/m) at point M, where MA = 1 cm and MB = 3 cm.

Two electric charges, q1 = 4.1 × 10^-10 C and q2 = −4.1 × 10^-10 C, are placed at points A and B in air, with AB = 2 cm. Determine the magnitude of the electric field E (in V/m) at point N, given that triangle NAB is an equilateral triangle.

Two electric charges, q1 = -10 × 10⁻⁸ C and q2 = 10 × 10⁻⁸ C, are placed at points A and B in air, with AB = 6 cm. Determine the electric field vector at point M, which lies on the perpendicular bisector of AB and is 4 cm from AB.

A right-angled triangle ABC has its right angle at A. The side lengths are AB = 30 cm, AC = 40 cm, and BC = 50 cm (the hypotenuse). Identical point charges, q = 10 × 10⁻⁹ C, are placed at each of the three vertices (A, B, and C). Determine the electric field vector at point H, which is the foot of the altitude from vertex A to the hypotenuse BC.

Two point charges q1 = +2 × 10^-6 C and q2 = −2 × 10^-6 C are placed at points A and B, separated by 6 cm in air. Calculate the net electric field strength at point M, which is on the line connecting A and B, 2 cm away from q1.
Two electric charges, q1 = +1 × 10^-6 C and q2 = +1 × 10^-6 C, are placed at points A and B, separated by 8 cm. Calculate the total (or net) electric field strength at the midpoint of AB.

Three equal, like-signed electric charges q = 2 × 10^-6 C are placed at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle with a side length of 6 cm. Calculate the electric field strength at the center of the triangle.

Two electric charges q1 = +3 × 10^-6 C and q2 = +4 × 10^-6 C are placed at points A and B, which are 5 cm apart. Calculate the resultant electric field strength at point M, which is 3 cm from A and 4 cm from B.

Two point charges q1 = −2 × 10^-6 C and q2 = −2 × 10^-6 C are placed at points A and B, 6 cm apart. Calculate the resultant electric field strength at point C, which lies on the perpendicular bisector of AB, at a distance of 4 cm from AB.

Two electric charges, q1 = +1 × 10^-6 C and q2 = −1 × 10^-6 C, are placed 10 cm apart. Calculate the resultant electric field at point M, which is equidistant from both charges and 5 cm away from the line segment connecting them.

Two electric charges, q1 = +3 × 10^-6 C and q2 = −3 × 10^-6 C, are placed at points A and B, separated by 10 cm. Point M lies on the perpendicular bisector of AB, at a distance of 6 cm from the line segment AB. Calculate the resultant electric field strength at M.

An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance C = 500 pF and is charged to a potential difference U = 300 V. It is then disconnected from the source and immersed in a liquid dielectric with dielectric constant ε = 2. What is the potential difference between the plates of the capacitor?

An air-filled parallel plate capacitor has a capacitance C = 500 pF and is charged to a voltage U = 300 V. While still connected to the source, it is immersed in a liquid dielectric with a dielectric constant ε = 2. What will be the potential difference between the capacitor plates?

An air-filled parallel plate capacitor with capacitance C = 500 pF is charged to a voltage U = 300 V. The capacitor is then disconnected from the source and immersed in a liquid dielectric with a relative permittivity (dielectric constant) of ε_r = 2. What is the electric field energy stored between the plates of the capacitor?

An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance C = 500 pF and is charged to a voltage U = 300 V. The capacitor remains connected to the voltage source while it is immersed in a liquid dielectric with a dielectric constant ε = 2. What is the electric field energy between the plates of the capacitor?
An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor with capacitance C = 2 pF is charged to a voltage U = 600 V. Calculate the charge Q of the capacitor.

An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor with capacitance C = 2 pF is charged to a voltage of U = 600 V. The capacitor is then disconnected from the source, and its plates are moved apart such that the distance between them is doubled. Calculate the new capacitance C1.

A parallel-plate air capacitor with a capacitance of C = 2pF is charged to a potential difference of U = 600 V. The capacitor is then disconnected from the power source, and its plates are moved apart so that the distance between them doubles. Calculate the new potential difference, U1.

An air-filled parallel plate capacitor with a capacitance C = 2 pF is charged to a potential difference U = 600 V. While still connected to the source, the plates are moved further apart so that the distance between them doubles. Calculate the new potential difference, U2.

A parallel-plate capacitor has circular plates with a radius of 10 cm. The distance between the plates and the potential difference across them are 1 cm and 108 V, respectively. The space between the plates is air. What is the charge on the capacitor?

A parallel-plate capacitor in air is connected to a 50 V power source. The capacitor is then disconnected from the source, and the distance between its plates is doubled. What is the voltage across the capacitor at that point?

A parallel plate capacitor has circular plates with a radius R = 60 cm. The distance between the plates is d = 2 mm. The space between the plates is filled with air. What is the capacitance of the capacitor?

A parallel-plate capacitor has circular plates with a radius R = 60 cm. The plate separation is d = 2 mm. Air is between the plates. What is the maximum charge that can be stored on the capacitor without dielectric breakdown? Given that the maximum electric field strength air can withstand is 3 x 10^5 V/m.

A parallel-plate capacitor with a capacitance of 7.0 nF is filled with a dielectric. The area of each plate is 15 cm² and the distance between the plates is 10⁻⁵ m. What is the dielectric constant of the dielectric?

Two capacitors with capacitances C1 = 2 μF and C2 = 3 μF are charged to voltages U1 = 200 V and U2 = 400 V, respectively. Afterwards, their like-charged plates are connected together. What is the voltage across the combined capacitors?
Two capacitors, C1 = 3μF and C2 = 6μF, are connected in series across a circuit segment AB with a total voltage UAB = 10 V. What is the voltage across capacitor C2?

Two capacitors with capacitances C1 = 0.4μF and C2 = 0.6μF are connected in parallel, and then connected to a power source with a voltage U < 60 V. One of the two capacitors has a charge of 3 × 10^-5 C. Calculate the voltage U.
A parallel-plate air capacitor has an area of each plate of 33.2 cm² and the distance between the two plates is 1.86 mm. Calculate the capacitance of the capacitor.
A parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 15.76 pF and is charged to a voltage of 91.6 V. Calculate the charge stored on the capacitor.
An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 15.76 pF and is charged to a voltage of 91.6 V. Calculate the electric field energy stored in the capacitor.
A parallel-plate air capacitor has a plate area of 19.6 cm² and a plate separation of 0.41 mm. Calculate its capacitance.
A parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 42.64 pF and is charged with a potential difference of 122.7 V. Calculate the electric charge that the capacitor stores.
A parallel-plate air capacitor has a capacitance of 42.64 pF and is charged to a potential difference of 122.7 V. Calculate the electric field energy stored in the capacitor.
A parallel plate air capacitor has a plate area of 44.1 cm² and the distance between the two plates is 0.79 mm. Calculate the capacitance of the capacitor.
A parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 49.11 pF and is charged to a voltage of 66.3 V. Calculate the charge accumulated by the capacitor.
An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 49.11 pF and is charged to a potential difference of 66.3 V. Calculate the electric field energy stored in the capacitor.
An air-filled parallel-plate capacitor has a plate area of 34.5 cm² and the distance between the two plates is 0.64 mm. Calculate the capacitance of the capacitor.
